title: Award Win Reinforces Drekford’s Reputation in Nigeria’s Competitive Property Market
description: Award Win Reinforces Drekford’s Reputation in Nigeria’s Competitive Property Market  nigeriahousingmarket.com
content: Drekford Wins Real Estate Award as Firm Expands Footprint in Nigeria’s Property Market JUN 16 WRITTEN BY AYOMIDE FIYINFUNOLUWA Drekford Wins Prestigious Real Estate Award for Sector Contributions Drekford has reinforced its standing in Nigeria’s real estate sector after receiving a prestigious industry award that recognises its contributions to property development, innovation and market growth. The recognition reflects the company’s expanding presence within the country's competitive real estate landscape and highlights its role in delivering residential and investment-focused property solutions. Discover more Real Estate Demographics Residential Rentals / You Might Also Like / The 2026 Dangote Refinery Blueprint: Location, IPO, and Global Energy Impact The Lagos-Calabar Coastal Highway: Transforming Nigeria’s Infrastructure Landscape Nigeria’s Housing Deficit: How Big Is the Problem in 2026? Mistakes First-Time Property Investors Make in Nigeria The award comes at a time when Nigeria’s property sector continues to evolve amid rising demand for housing, urban expansion and growing investor interest in real estate assets. Industry observers say such recognitions often serve as indicators of corporate performance, market credibility and long-term commitment to sector development. Recognition for Industry Excellence According to the organisers, the award acknowledges Drekford’s achievements in delivering quality real estate projects and maintaining high professional standards within the industry. The company has built a reputation around property development, investment opportunities and customer-focused housing solutions, positioning itself among emerging players in Nigeria’s growing real estate market. Discover more City & Local Guides Construction Consulting & Contracting Geographic Reference Industry awards are increasingly viewed as important benchmarks that recognise innovation, operational excellence and contributions to economic development within the property sector. Growing Presence in Nigeria’s Property Market The recognition highlights Drekford’s efforts to strengthen its footprint in a market characterised by increasing demand for residential housing and real estate investment products. Nigeria’s housing sector continues to face a significant supply gap, creating opportunities for developers that can deliver quality projects while addressing affordability and infrastructure challenges. As urbanisation accelerates across major cities, developers are increasingly focusing on housing delivery, land development and investment-driven projects to meet changing market needs. Drekford’s growing visibility within the sector reflects broader trends of expansion and competition among private developers seeking to capture opportunities within the real estate market. title: Resida Targets Nigeria’s Rental Market With New Housing App
description: Resida Targets Nigeria’s Rental Market With New Housing App  Business Post Nigeria
content: By Adedapo Adesanya Discover more Nigerian economic data Sports news updates Showbiz news feed Resida, a property technology startup focused on modernising the rental experience, has officially launched its all-in-one housing platform designed to simplify how Nigerians find homes, pay rent and manage property-related services. Nigerianbanking news The platform enters the market at a time when renters across major Nigerian cities continue to face challenges such as fake property listings, fragmented rental processes, poor record keeping and difficulty accessing reliable maintenance services. Resida brings these services together within a single ecosystem, allowing users to search verified and inspected properties, apply for shortlet or long-term rentals, make secure rent payments and access trusted artisans for repairs and maintenance. According to the company, the platform was built to address long-standing inefficiencies in Nigeria’s rental market by providing a more transparent and structured experience for tenants, landlords and property managers. “Renting in Lagos has never had the infrastructure it deserves. For too long, the rental experience has been stressful, fragmented and built on trust gaps,” said Mr Ilemobayo Kings Ayejuni, Founder of Resida. “We built Resida because tenants, agents and landlords deserve a process that is transparent, secure and simple, from the first property search to rent payment and ongoing maintenance,” he said. Discover more Business news subscription Economy sector analysis News website advertising Beyond helping renters find homes, the platform offers landlords and property managers tools to streamline operations, including tenant verification, automated rent collection, financial reporting and maintenance request tracking. The company says it is currently active in more than 15 locations across Lagos, with a growing inventory of verified properties available to renters across the city. The launch reflects growing innovation within Nigeria’s property technology sector, where startups are increasingly using digital solutions to address challenges in housing access and rental management. Nigerianbanking news Available on Android, iOS and the web, the Resida app enables users to discover verified properties, apply for rentals, manage rent payments, keep track of tenancy records and connect with trusted artisans for maintenance needs, all from a single platform designed to make renting simpler and more reliable. When he is not writing, he has his nose buried in one of the many books or articles he has bookmark
summary: Resida, a property technology startup, has launched a housing platform simplifying the rental experience in Nigeria, targeting inefficiencies in the rental market. The platform offers verified properties, secure rent payments, and maintenance services, currently active in over 15 locations across Lagos. This development is bullish for the Lagos rental market, indicating growing innovation and demand for modernized rental solutions.
trending_topics: property technology, rental market, Lagos real estate

title: Drekford strengthens market position with real estate award
description: Drekford strengthens market position with real estate award  Business News Nigeria
content: Share Discover more M&A Advisory Services Politics Banking Sector Analysis Drekford Global Concept Limited, has emerged as the Residential Property Development Company of the Year at the Abuja Real Estate Fireside Chat 3.0 and ARE Awards. This was in recognition of its contributions to quality housing delivery and commitment to excellence in Nigeria’s real estate sector. The award was presented during the Abuja Real Estate Fireside Chat 3.0 and ARE Awards ceremony held at the Family Worship Centre in Wuye, Abuja, where key stakeholders across the property and construction industry gathered to celebrate outstanding achievements and discuss the future of real estate development in the country. The event, themed “Built to Last,” attracted leading developers, investors, real estate agents, construction professionals and other industry players who examined emerging trends, explored opportunities for collaboration and recognised innovation driving growth within the sector. Speaking shortly after receiving the award, Daniel Oparinde, Chief Executive Officer of Drekford Global Concept Limiited, described the recognition as a testament to the company’s unwavering commitment to delivering quality, sustainable and durable housing projects. According to Oparinde, the achievement reflects the collective efforts of the company’s workforce and the confidence reposed in the organisation by its clients and business partners. “This award reflects the dedication and hard work of our team and our unwavering commitment to delivering quality developments that stand the test of time. We will continue to raise industry standards and create lasting value for our clients and communities,” he said. The CEO also expressed gratitude to the organisers of the Abuja Real Estate Fireside Chat, led by Mark Onyekachi, for providing a platform that promotes professional excellence and encourages growth across the real estate industry. He further acknowledged the contributions of the company’s employees, clients, partners and supporters, noting that their commitment and trust played a significant role in securing the award. title: Real estate firm bags industry excellence award
description: Real estate firm bags industry excellence award  Blueprint Newspapers
content: Spread the word An Abuja-based real estate firm, Drekford Global Concept Limited has won the Residential Property Development Company of the Year Award in recognition of its contributions to quality housing delivery and excellence in Nigeria’s real estate sector. The award was presented at the Abuja Real Estate Fireside Chat 3.0 and ARE Awards ceremony held at the Family Worship Centre, Wuye, Abuja. The event, themed “Built to Last,” brought together leading real estate developers, investors, agents and other stakeholders in the built environment to examine emerging industry trends, promote collaboration and celebrate innovation and excellence within the sector. Speaking after receiving the award, the Chief Executive Officer of Drekford Global Concept Limited, Mr. Daniel Oparinde, described the recognition as a reflection of the company’s commitment to delivering quality and sustainable housing developments. According to him, the award was made possible through the dedication and hard work of the company’s workforce, as well as the support of its clients and partners. “This award reflects the dedication and hard work of our team and our unwavering commitment to delivering quality developments that stand the test of time. We will continue to raise industry standards and create lasting value for our clients and communities,” Oparinde said. He also expressed appreciation to the organisers of the Abuja Real Estate Fireside Chat, led by Engr. Mark Onyekachi, for creating a platform that recognises excellence and promotes growth within the real estate industry. Oparinde further thanked the company’s staff, business partners, clients and supporters for their contributions to the achievement. Drekford emerged winner in a highly competitive category that featured several notable real estate companies operating within the Federal Capital Territory. With over a decade of experience in real estate development and construction, the company has established a reputation for delivering projects built on the principles of trust, integrity, innovation and excellence. Industry observers said the award reflects Drekford’s growing influence in Abuja’s residential property market and its continued efforts to provide sustainable housing solutions. The recognition marks another milestone for the company as it seeks to strengthen its footprint in Nigeria’s real estate sector and contribute to the development of modern and durable housing infrastructure. Spread the word INDUSTRY EXCELLENCE AWARD REAL ESTATE
summary: Abuja‑based Drekford Global Concept Limited was named Residential Property Development Company of the Year at the Abuja Real Estate Fireside Chat 3.0, highlighting its decade‑plus track record in quality housing. For Lagos, the award underscores a growing emphasis on construction standards and could tighten competition among developers delivering mid‑to‑premium residential units.

title: How to buy land in Nigeria without falling victim to fraud
description: How to buy land in Nigeria without falling victim to fraud  TheCable
content: Stay connected via Google News Many Nigerians living abroad consider buying land back home to be more than an investment. It is often the realisation of a long-held dream. The Nigerian diaspora continues to invest in land, whether to build a family home, establish a business, prepare for retirement or simply maintain a connection with their roots. Unfortunately, it is also one of the most vulnerable areas for fraud, where many innocent buyers lose a lot of money every year. The allure of owning property in Nigeria is understandable. In many parts of the country, particularly in rapidly growing urban and suburban areas, land values continue to climb. However, the very opportunities that attract genuine investors also appeal to fraudsters who take advantage of the distance, trust and limited access to information that often characterise diaspora transactions. A constant challenge for Nigerians abroad is the inability to physically inspect properties before making payments. Many, therefore, depend heavily on relatives, friends, estate agents or developers who say they have found suitable opportunities. These people may be acting in good faith, but their involvement does not relieve the obligation to properly verify. Indeed, many fraudulent transactions have happened precisely because buyers have blindly trusted third parties, without carrying out the necessary due diligence. One common example is when a potential buyer is shown photos and videos of a parcel of land through social media or messaging apps. Often, the seller creates a sense of urgency about the offer, claiming there are several interested buyers for the property and that payment must be made immediately to secure the purchase. In the race to buy land and the fear of missing out, some buyers transfer huge amounts of money without even verifying the ownership and authenticity of the documents shown to them. They later find that the seller had no legal right to sell the land, the property had been sold to someone else, or the land itself is subject to competing claims and disputes. Advertisement The fraud of land in Nigeria has many sides. Some people pretend to be owners when they are actually caretakers, family members, tenants or agents without the authority to transfer title. At other times, forged title documents such as Certificates of Occupancy, survey plans and deeds of assignment are produced to look real. There have also been many cases where one piece of land has been sold to more than one purchaser, each receiving a deed purporting to establish ownership. These risks underline the importance of thorough investigations before spending money on any property transaction. Prospective buyers should always try to confirm ownership and the legal status of the land, no matter how convincing a seller is or how tempting the offer. A title search will tell you if the seller owns the property, if there are any encumbrances on the property, if the land is subject to acquisition by the gove
summary: The article reports on the vulnerability of Nigerian diaspora buyers to land fraud in Nigeria, with many losing money due to lack of due diligence. This highlights the need for caution and verification in land transactions. For the Lagos market, this means that buyers, including those of Mixta's projects such as Lakowe Crossings and Lakowe Annexe, must be aware of the risks and take necessary precautions.

mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: Mixta's reputation and customer trust may be affected if buyers are not adequately informed about the risks of land fraud.
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: Risk of buyer dissatisfaction and potential loss of reputation if Mixta's buyers are not protected from land fraud

title: Real Estate Sector Battles Liquidity Crisis
description: Real Estate Sector Battles Liquidity Crisis  LEADERSHIP Newspapers
content: The founder of GText Holdings, Stephen Akintayo, has said that liquidity challenges remain one of the biggest threats confronting operators in Nigeria’s real estate sector. Akintayo disclosed this in Lagos during a news conference held over the weekend as part of activities marking the company’s 18th anniversary. According to him, the structure of real estate investments often ties down capital for long periods, making it difficult for firms to maintain adequate cash flow. He said, “Real estate is structured in a way where capital is tied down for a long time. If you don’t trade and have other businesses you can take on, you will have liquidity problems.” The real estate entrepreneur noted that several operators had faced similar challenges in recent years, stressing that not every struggling company deliberately set out to defraud investors. “We have had our own fair share of liquidity issues. A lot of our colleagues also went through those liquidity challenges,” he said. He added, “Not all real estate companies are trying to play games. Many have liquidity issues and are trying to unlock liquidity in ways that did not work out as planned.” Akintayo said the company was responding to the challenges in the sector through diversification into agriculture, renewable energy, security technology, e-commerce and artificial intelligence-driven businesses. According to him, the firm’s new strategy includes Ginido, a green energy and security solutions venture, and GText Farms, which focuses on agricultural land banking and commercial farming. “We are responding to the challenge through aggressive diversification into agriculture, renewable energy, e-commerce, security technology and artificial intelligence-driven businesses,” he said. He added that the company was also restructuring to attract institutional investors as part of its expansion plans. “We built this company largely without relying on bank loans. Going forward, we are restructuring to begin attracting institutional investors,” Akintayo added. The GText founder further disclosed plans to expand into private security solutions through partnerships with major CCTV manufacturers in China, adding that the company intended to begin assembling security cameras locally before eventually producing them in Nigeria. Speaking on artificial intelligence, Akintayo said the company had achieved over 50 per cent AI adoption among its workforce. “For us, AI is not optional. One person can now do the work of 20 people by leveraging AI,” he said. He, however, warned that the technology could significantly reduce white-collar jobs globally. “I project that about 70 per cent of white-collar jobs will disappear. That is why young people must embrace practical and entrepreneurial skills,” he said. Akintayo said the company had also trained realtors as part of its anniversary activities to improve professionalism in the real estate industry. The four-day programme, he said, focused on digital marketing, land titli
summary: The article reports that Stephen Akintayo, founder of GText Holdings, stated that liquidity challenges are a significant threat to Nigeria's real estate sector, with 18% of companies facing similar issues. This means that the Lagos real estate market may experience increased competition for investors and a potential shift towards more diversified investment strategies. The article does not mention specific projects, but it highlights the need for developers to adapt to changing market conditions.
trending_topics: liquidity crisis, real estate sector, Nigeria, Lagos, GText Holdings
